content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Jesse D. Gutierrez 
Broker/Owner CalBRE #0117448 
Vision Real Estate Group 
716 Laurel Street, Suite I 
San Carlos, CA 94070 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-15-156 
Dear Mr. Gutierrez: 
November 5, 2015 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Refonn Act (the "Act").1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the 
conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions 
such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Moreover, we base this letter on the facts 
presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it renders 
advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) 
QUESTION 
Do the Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit you from taking part in decisions 
reęarding infill projects located within 500 feet of property your company is currently subleasing to 
another company and more than 1,000 feet from your company's current location? 
CONCLUSION 
Considering the small scale of both infill projects and their consistency with the existing 
neighborhood, neither proposed project appears to have a reasonably foreseeable material effect on 
your interests in your business, the leased property, or the company subleasing the leased property. 
Barring additional facts, the Act does not prohibit you from taking part in the decisions regarding 
the infill projects. 
FACTS 
You are a planning commissioner with the City of San Carlos. The planning commission has 
received applications for two infill projects (530 Walriut Avenue and 545 Walnut Avenue) near 
commercial property leased by your company, which you are currently subleasing to another tenant, 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-15-156 
Page No. 2 
and your current office. Your corporation, Finisterra Financial, Inc., is as California S Corporation 
and you own all shares in the corporation. Finisterra was established in 2003 and is a real estate 
brokerage. It fonnerly operated under the name RE/MAX Vision Real Estate and is currently 
operated under the name Vision Real Estate Group. Property leased by Finisterra at 530 El Camino 
Real, from Figone Properties, was the former location of RE/MAX Vision Real Estate. However, 
Finisterra has since subleased the location to Lawyers Title Company, tenninated its franchise 
agreement with RE/MAX, and relocated the brokerage to 716 Laurel Street, which is more than 
1,000 feet from the infill projects.2 
Lawyers Title Company is a subsidiary of Fidelity National Financial, Inc., a family of 
companies that "collectively represents the largest title insurance and escrow services company in 
the world." Finisterra's lease provides two options that it may exercise extending the lease until 
2023. The sublease with Lawyers Title Company provides for an initial tenn until 2018 and an 
option for Lawyers Title Company to extend the tenn until 2023. Finisterra's rental rate through 
2023 has been established in its lease with Figone Properties. Lawyers Title Company's rental rate 
through 2023 has been established in the sublease. 
530 Walnut Avenue is just over 500 feet from 530 El Camino Real. The application for this 
project proposes replacing a single-family home with a 9-unit apartment complex with ten parking 
places. 
545 Walnut Avenue is just under 500 feet from 530 El Camino Real. The application for this 
project proposes replacing three existing apartment units with a mixed-use project consisting of a 
multistory building with approximately 1,000 square feet of commercial space and nine apartment 
units with 12 covered parking spaces. 
Walnut A venue divides a Mixed-Use Zoning District and a Multi-Family Zoning District. 
The neighborhood consists of a mix of uses including several single-family homes, multi-unit 
apartments, a dentist office, and the headquarters for the San Mateo County Transit District. Walnut 
A venue intersects San Carlos A venue, a main thoroughfare through the city, and is close walking 
distance to downtown shops and restaurants. El Camino Real is two blocks from Walnut Avenue. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
(Section 87103.) A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable financial 
effect of a governmental decision on a public official's interests is material. Different standards 
apply to determine whether a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on an interest will be material 
depending on the nature of the interest. Interests from which a conflict of interest may arise are 
defined in Section 87103. Interests implicated by your account of the facts are the following: 
2 While you have not provided any information regarding the lease or ownership of 716 Laurel A venue, you 
may also have an interest in this property. However, to the extent that you have a leasehold interest in the property, we 
find no reasonably foreseeable effect on the interest for the same reasons we find no reasonable foreseeable effect on 
your leasehold interest in 530 El Camino Real. (Regulation I 8702.2(b).) To the extent that you own the property, we 
find no reasonably foreseeable effect on the income producing potential or market value of the property for the same 
reasons we find no foreseeable effect on you interest in Finisterra. (Regulation l 8702.2(a)(8) and (12).) 


File No. A-15-156 
Page No. 3 
• Finisterra: Under Section 87103(a) and (d), an official has an interest in any business 
entity in which the official has an investment of $2,000 or more. An official also has an 
interest in any source of income aggregating $500 or more within 12 months prior to the 
time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) As the sole owner of the real estate 
brokerage, you have an interest in Finisterra as a business entity. You also have an 
interest in Finisterra as a source of income.3 
• Lawyers Title Company: Under Section 82030(a), if a public official owns a 10-percent 
or greater interest in a business, a source of income to that business is also a source of 
income to the public official. As the sole owner of Finisterra, you have an interest in 
Lawyers Title Company as a source of income resulting from the rent received for the 
lease of 530 El Camino Real. (Section 87103(c).) 
• 530 El Camino Real: An "interest in real property" includes "any leasehold, beneficial 
or ownership interest or an option to acquire such an interest in real property located in 
the jurisdiction owned directly, indirectly or beneficially by the public official, or other 
filer, or his or her immediate family if the fair market value of the interest is two 
thousand dollars ($2,000) or more.'' (Section 82033.)4 This includes the pro rata share of 
any interest in real property of any business entity in which the official has a l 0-percent 
or greater interest. (Ibid.) As the sole owner of Finisterra, which has an interest in its 
lease with Figone with a value exceeding $2,000, you have a real property interest 530 
El Camino Real. (Section 87103(b).) 
Foreseeability 
Generally, a financial effect is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable if the interest is a 
named party in, or the subject of, a governmental decision before the official or the official's 
agency. (Regulation l8701(a).) A financial interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision 
involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other 
entitlement to, or contract with, the financial interest, and includes any governmental decision 
affecting a real property financial interest as described in Regulation 18702.2(a)( I )-(6). 
Based upon the facts provided, none of your interest will be explicitly involved in decisions 
regarding either of the infill projects. Because your interests are "not explicitly involved" in the 
decisions, a financial effect is reasonably foreseeable if the effect can be recognized as a realistic 
possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. A financial effect need not be likely to be 
considered reasonably foreseeable. (Regulation 1870 l (b ). ) 
3 We note that you also have a potential interest in any customer of Finisterra as a sources of incoine (Section 
87030) as well as interests in any sources of commission income received for services rendered as a broker, agent, or 
sales person as a real estate broker (See Regulation 18700. l .) To the extent that there may be a reasonably foreseeable 
material effect on any customer of Finisterra or source of commission income, the conclusions reached in the letter do 
not apply. If a customer ofFinisterra or source of commission income is implicated by the decision you will need to 
seek additional advice. 
[ An interest in real property does not include a periodic tenancy of one month or less. (Regulation 18233.) 


Materiality 
File No. A-15-156 
Page No. 4 
A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable impact of a 
governmental decision on a public official's interests is material. (Regulation 18700(a).) Different 
standards apply to determine whether a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on an interest will be 
material depending on the nature of the interest. 
Finisterra and Lawyers Title Company: A reasonably foreseeable effect on an interest in a 
business entity, including any business entity that is a source income, not explicitly involved in the 
decision is material "if a prudent person with sufficient information would find it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision's financial effect would contribute to a change in the price of the 
business entity's publicly traded stock, or the value of a privately-held business entity." Examples 
of decisions with material effects include decisions that: 
• Authorize, prohibit, regulate, or establish conditions for an activity in which the business 
is engaged. 
• 
Increase or decrease competition for the business. 
• 
Increase or decrease the need for the products or services supplied by the business. 
• 
Improve the surrounding neighborhood in a manner that may affect the amount of 
business the business receives. 
• 
Decide a location of a major development, entertainment facility, or project, which 
would increase or decrease the amount of business the business receives. 
• 
Increase or decrease the tax burden, debt, or financial or legal liability of the business. 
(Regulation 18702.l(b).) 
In this case, neither infill project is of the size or scope that would have far-reaching 
financial effects. Both projects replace existing residential units, including an existing three-unit 
apartment complex, and are consistent with comparable properties within the neighborhood, which 
already includes a mix of small apartment complexes and other commercial properties. Combined, 
the projects will result in just 14 additional housing units and approximately l 000 square feet of 
commercial space. Moreover, neither Finisterra nor Lawyers Title Company is a retail business and 
neither appears to rely heavily on foot-traffic. It is unlikely that the projects will significantly affect 
customer accessibility or the amount of business either business receives. For these reasons, we find 
that decisions regarding the projects will not have a reasonably foreseeable material on either your 
interest in Finisterra o_r Lawyers Title Company. 
530 El Camino Real: For a real property interest in a lease, current Regulation 18705.2(b) 
states, with limited exceptions, that the "reasonably foreseeable financial effects of a governmental 
decision on any real property in which a governmental official has a leasehold financial interest is 
material" if the decision will: 


"(l) Change the termination date of the lease; 
"(2) Increase or decrease the potential _rental value of the property; 
File No. A-15-1 56 
Page No. 5 
"(3) Increase or decrease the rental value of the property, and the official has 
a right to sublease the property; 
"(4) Change the official's actual or legally allowable use of the real property; 
"(5) Impact the official's use and enjoyment of the real property." 
Once again considering the small scale of both infill projects and their consistency with the 
existing neighborhood, there is no reason to believe either project will have a reasonably 
foreseeable material effect on your leasehold interest in 5 30 El Camino Real. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 32 2-566 0. 
BGL:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
Brian G. Lau 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

reęarding infill projects located within 500 feet of property your company is currently subleasing to 
another company and more than 1,000 feet from your company's current location?

Considering the small scale of both infill projects and their consistency with the existing 
neighborhood, neither proposed project appears to have a reasonably foreseeable material effect on 
your interests in your business, the leased property, or the company subleasing the leased property. 
Barring additional facts, the Act does not prohibit you from taking part in the decisions regarding 
the infill projects.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit you from taking part in decisions 
reęarding infill projects located within 500 feet of property your company is currently subleasing to 
another company and more than 1,000 feet from your company's current location?

CONCLUSION: Considering the small scale of both infill projects and their consistency with the existing 
neighborhood, neither proposed project appears to have a reasonably foreseeable material effect on 
your interests in your business, the leased property, or the company subleasing the leased property. 
Barring additional facts, the Act does not prohibit you from taking part in the decisions regarding 
the infill projects.
